---
title: "Trial_edgeR_script"
author: "Aravind Sundaravadivelu"
date: "2026-02-11"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(edgeR)
library(data.table)
library(dplyr)
library(gplots)
require(DESeq)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r}
Genewisecounts<- fread("/home/tmhaxs421/brannanlab/VS_share/VS_AS_folder/Clip_analysis/analysis/MassSpec_data/IgGoverShortTertIR.csv")
dim(Genewisecounts)
```

```{r}
dup_tbl <- Genewisecounts %>%
  count(Genes, sort = TRUE) %>%
  filter(n > 1)

genes_removed <- dup_tbl$Genes

cat(
  sprintf(
    "The following %d genes that are repeated are removed: (%s).\n",
    length(genes_removed),
    paste(genes_removed, collapse = ", ")
  )
)

Genewisecounts_dedup<-Genewisecounts %>% distinct(Genes, .keep_all = TRUE)
Genewisecounts_dedup
```


```{r}
gene_id <- Genewisecounts_dedup[[1]]
counts  <- as.matrix(Genewisecounts_dedup[, -1, with = FALSE])
rownames(counts) <- gene_id

# first 2 columns = control, last 2 = experiment
group <- factor(c(rep("control", 2), rep("experiment", 2)),
                levels = c("control", "experiment"))

dge <- DGEList(counts = counts, group = group)

# ---- split gene + counts ----
gene_id <- df[[1]]  # first column is gene
counts  <- as.matrix(df[, -1, with = FALSE])
rownames(counts) <- gene_id

# make sure counts are numeric
storage.mode(counts) <- "numeric"

# ---- group labels: first 2 = control, last 2 = experiment ----
group <- factor(c(rep("control", 2), rep("experiment", 2)),
                levels = c("control", "experiment"))

stopifnot(ncol(counts) == length(group))  # sanity check

# ---- edgeR object ----
y <- DGEList(counts = counts, group = group)

# ---- filter low signal ----
# keep <- filterByExpr(y, group = group)
# y <- y[keep, , keep.lib.sizes = FALSE]

# ---- normalize ----
y <- calcNormFactors(y)

# ---- design matrix ----
design <- model.matrix(~ group)  # includes intercept (control baseline)
# coefficient of interest will be: groupexperiment

# ---- estimate dispersion ----
y <- estimateDisp(y, design)

# ---- fit + test (robust with small n) ----
fit <- glmQLFit(y, design, robust = TRUE)
res <- glmQLFTest(fit, coef = "groupexperiment")  # experiment vs control

# ---- results table ----
tab <- topTags(res, n = Inf)$table
tab$Genes <- rownames(tab)

# # save
# fwrite(as.data.table(tab), file = "edgeR_experiment_vs_control.tsv", sep = "\t")
# 
# # look at top hits
# head(tab, 20)
```

```{r}

cols <- names(Genewisecounts_dedup)[2:5]

long <- df[, ..cols] |>
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = "sample", values_to = "value") |>
  transform(value = as.numeric(value))

ggplot(long, aes(x = value)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50) +
  facet_wrap(~ sample, scales = "free_x") +
  theme_bw()
```

```{r}

genes <- Genewisecounts_dedup[[1]]
sample_cols <- names(Genewisecounts_dedup)[2:5]          # first 2 control, last 2 experiment
X <- as.matrix(Genewisecounts_dedup[, ..sample_cols])
X <- apply(X, 2, as.numeric)
rownames(X) <- genes

# If values are NOT already log2, log2-transform.
# Heuristic: if values are large (e.g. > 100) it's probably intensity, so log2 it.
if (max(X, na.rm=TRUE) > 100) {
  X <- log2(X + 1)
}

group <- factor(c(rep("control", 2), rep("experiment", 2)),
                levels=c("control","experiment"))

design <- model.matrix(~0 + group)
colnames(design) <- levels(group)

fit <- lmFit(X, design)
contr <- makeContrasts(experiment_vs_control = experiment - control, levels=design)
fit2 <- contrasts.fit(fit, contr)
fit2 <- eBayes(fit2)

res1 <- topTable(fit2, coef="experiment_vs_control", number=Inf, sort.by="P")
# res$Genes <- rownames(res)
# 
# res[1:20, c("Genes","logFC","P.Value","adj.P.Val")]

res1

```


```{r}
# 1) gene/protein IDs (first col) + data matrix (cols 2:5)
genes <- Genewisecounts[[1]]
X <- as.matrix(Genewisecounts[, 2:5, with = FALSE])
X <- apply(X, 2, as.numeric)
rownames(X) <- genes
X_raw <- X
X_norm <- sweep(X_raw, 2, apply(X_raw, 2, median, na.rm = TRUE), FUN = "-")

par(mfrow = c(2,2))

# Medians (after should be ~0 by definition)
barplot(apply(X_raw,  2, median, na.rm=TRUE), las=2, main="Median (raw)", ylab="median")
barplot(apply(X_norm, 2, median, na.rm=TRUE), las=2, main="Median (median-centered)", ylab="median")

# Spread summaries (these actually show a change if present)
barplot(apply(X_raw,  2, IQR, na.rm=TRUE), las=2, main="IQR (raw)", ylab="IQR")
barplot(apply(X_norm, 2, IQR, na.rm=TRUE), las=2, main="IQR (median-centered)", ylab="IQR")

par(mfrow = c(1,1))

par(mfrow = c(1,2))
boxplot(X_raw,  las=2, main="Before", ylab="value")
boxplot(X_norm, las=2, main="After median-centering", ylab="value")
par(mfrow = c(1,1))


library(limma)
par(mfrow = c(1,2))
plotDensities(X_raw,  main="Densities: before")
plotDensities(X_norm, main="Densities: after median-centering")
par(mfrow = c(1,1))


# 2) OPTIONAL: median-center each sample so the median log2FC is 0
# (recommended if you believe it's "non-normalized")
X <- sweep(X, 2, apply(X, 2, median, na.rm = TRUE), FUN = "-")

# Alternative mild normalization via limma (single-channel style):
# X <- normalizeBetweenArrays(X, method = "scale")  # uses limma normalization methods :contentReference[oaicite:2]{index=2}

# 3) design: first 2 columns control, last 2 experiment
group <- factor(c("control","control","experiment","experiment"),
                levels = c("control","experiment"))

design <- model.matrix(~0 + group)
colnames(design) <- levels(group)

# 4) fit + contrast (experiment - control)
fit <- lmFit(X, design)
contr <- makeContrasts(experiment_vs_control = experiment - control, levels = design)
fit2 <- contrasts.fit(fit, contr)

# 5) moderated stats
fit2 <- eBayes(fit2, robust = TRUE)

# 6) results
res2 <- topTable(fit2, coef = "experiment_vs_control", number = Inf, sort.by = "P")
# res$Genes <- rownames(res)
# 
# res[1:20, c("Genes","logFC","P.Value","adj.P.Val")]
res2

```

```{r}
# library(data.table)
# library(dplyr)
# library(ggplot2)
# library(ggpubr)
# 
# # Correlation plot
# res1_sub <- res1 %>% 
#   select(logFC) %>% 
#   rownames_to_column(var = "ID")
# 
# res2_sub <-  res2 %>% 
#   select(logFC) %>% 
#   rownames_to_column(var = "ID")
# 
# res <- inner_join(res1_sub, res2_sub, by = "ID") 
# 
# print(cor.test(res$logFC.x, res$logFC.y, method = "pearson"))
# # print(cor.test(m$log2fc_1, m$log2fc_2, method = "spearman"))
# 
# # --- dot plot + regression line + correlation annotation ---
# ggplot(res, aes(x = logFC.x, y = logFC.y)) +
#   geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 1) +
#   geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +                 # :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}install.packages("ggpubr")
#   stat_cor(method = "pearson") +                           # :contentReference[oaicite:2]{index=2}
#   theme_bw() +
#   labs(x = "log2FC (table 1)", y = "log2FC (table 2)",
#        title = sprintf("Common genes: %d", nrow(res)))
```

```{r}
# # Correlation plot
# res1_sub <- res1 %>% 
#   select(adj.P.Val) %>% 
#   rownames_to_column(var = "ID")
# 
# res2_sub <-  res2 %>% 
#   select(adj.P.Val) %>% 
#   rownames_to_column(var = "ID")
# 
# res <- inner_join(res1_sub, res2_sub, by = "ID") 
# 
# print(cor.test(res$adj.P.Val.x, res$adj.P.Val.y, method = "pearson"))
# # print(cor.test(m$log2fc_1, m$log2fc_2, method = "spearman"))
# 
# # --- dot plot + regression line + correlation annotation ---
# ggplot(res, aes(x = adj.P.Val.x, y = adj.P.Val.y)) +
#   geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 1) +
#   geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +                 # :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}install.packages("ggpubr")
#   stat_cor(method = "pearson") +                           # :contentReference[oaicite:2]{index=2}
#   theme_bw() +
#   labs(x = "log2FC (table 1)", y = "log2FC (table 2)",
#        title = sprintf("Common genes: %d", nrow(res)))
```

```{r}
res_volcano <- res1 %>% 
  filter(logFC >= 1 ) %>% 
  filter(P.Value <= 0.05)

res1 <- res1 %>%
  mutate(
    Genes = if ("Genes" %in% names(.)) Genes else rownames(.),
    neglog10P = -log10(P.Value),
    status = case_when(
      adj.P.Val <= 0.05 & logFC >=  1 ~ "Up",
      adj.P.Val <= 0.05 & logFC <= -1 ~ "Down",
      TRUE ~ "NS"
    )
  )

# label a few top hits (optional)
top_labs <- res1 %>%
  filter(status != "NS") %>%
  arrange(adj.P.Val) %>%
  slice_head(n = 20)

ggplot(res1, aes(x = logFC, y = neglog10P, color = status)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 1.3) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-1, 1), linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed") +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = top_labs,
    aes(label = Genes),
    size = 3,
    max.overlaps = Inf
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x = "log2 Fold Change (logFC)", y = "-log10(P-value)", color = "")

```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)

res1 <- res1 %>%
  mutate(
    Genes = if ("Genes" %in% names(.)) Genes else rownames(.),
    # avoid Inf if adj.P.Val is 0
    neglog10FDR = -log10(adj.P.Val),
    status = case_when(
      adj.P.Val <= 0.05 & logFC >=  1 ~ "Up",
      adj.P.Val <= 0.05 & logFC <= -1 ~ "Down",
      TRUE ~ "NS"
    )
  )

top_labs <- res1 %>%
  filter(status != "NS") %>%
  arrange(adj.P.Val) %>%
  slice_head(n = 20)

ggplot(res1, aes(x = logFC, y = neglog10FDR, color = status)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 1.3) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-1, 1), linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed") +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = top_labs,
    aes(label = Genes),
    size = 3,
    max.overlaps = Inf
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = "log2 Fold Change (logFC)",
    y = "-log10(FDR)  (i.e., -log10(adj.P.Val))",
    color = ""
  )

```

```{r}
# BiocManager::install("EnhancedVolcano")  # if needed
library(EnhancedVolcano)

res2$Genes <- if ("Genes" %in% names(res1)) res1$Genes else rownames(res1)

EnhancedVolcano(
  res2,
  lab = res2$Genes,
  x = "logFC",
  y = "P.Value",        # or "adj.P.Val" # or "P.Value"
  FCcutoff = 1,
  pCutoff = 0.05
)

```
